symptoms.symptom_name: New rapidly progressive bilateral temporal (outer) visual-field loss

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bitemporal pattern localises to the optic chiasm, where a suprasellar pituitary mass compresses the crossing nasal fibres first.
A fixed, bilateral temporal-field defect is not a feature of migrainous visual aura, which is typically transient and hemifield/scintillating.

symptoms.symptom_name: Confirmed dense bitemporal hemianopia on confrontation visual-field testing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An objectively confirmed bitemporal hemianopia is the classic mass-effect sign of chiasmal compression — sealed clinician measurement the AI Doctor cannot obtain; it must infer compression from the reported bilateral outer-field loss.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe acute worsening of a chronic frontal/retro-orbital headache

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sellar expansion stretches the diaphragma sellae and dura, causing headache; acute severe worsening raises apoplexy.
Sudden severe headache with rapid visual deterioration is the hallmark of haemorrhage/infarction into a pituitary tumour.
Bilateral, spontaneous, milky discharge reflects prolactin-driven lactogenesis; bilaterality argues against local breast pathology.
Papilloma classically causes unilateral, single-duct, often blood-stained discharge — not bilateral milky discharge.
Prolactin suppresses GnRH, lowering gonadotrophins and causing amenorrhoea.

symptoms.symptom_name: Loss of libido and two-year infertility

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypogonadotrophic hypogonadism from hyperprolactinaemia reduces libido and causes anovulatory infertility.
Absence of dopamine-antagonist drugs removes the commonest secondary (non-tumour) cause of hyperprolactinaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, neck stiffness or vomiting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduces (but does not exclude) meningitis and some intracranial catastrophes; apoplexy can still occur without these features.

investigation_details.expected_result: markedly elevated (a very high level favours a prolactin-secreting macroadenoma over stalk-effect hyperprolactinaemia)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Fails to characterise the endocrine driver and to distinguish a prolactinoma from a non-functioning mass with stalk effect.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Very high prolactin correlates with tumour burden in macroprolactinomas — hospital venous blood test, never available in real-time telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: MRI brain/pituitary showing a macroadenoma with suprasellar extension abutting the optic chiasm

investigation_details.expected_result: sellar mass >10 mm with suprasellar extension and optic chiasm compression; assess for haemorrhage (apoplexy)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The compressive lesion and apoplexy are not identified; sight-saving decompression could be delayed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Imaging is the definitive structural test — hospital-based and impossible via telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Galactorrhoea + amenorrhoea + low libido/infertility together strongly indicate significant hyperprolactinaemia and warrant prolactin measurement and pituitary imaging.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Pituitary macroadenoma with acute optic chiasm compression / possible apoplexy
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: New bilateral outer-field loss plus severe acute headache is a sight-threatening emergency requiring same-night imaging and specialist review.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent MRI brain and pituitary (with contrast)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Defines the mass, its suprasellar extension and chiasmal compression, and identifies haemorrhage (apoplexy); must be same-night given progressive visual loss.
Confirms and quantifies hyperprolactinaemia; a very high level supports a prolactin-secreting macroadenoma.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Anterior pituitary hormone profile (cortisol/ACTH, TSH/free T4, LH/FSH, oestradiol, IGF-1)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Assesses for hypopituitarism (especially cortisol deficiency, which is life-threatening in apoplexy) and co-secretion.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Formal visual-field testing (perimetry) and ophthalmology review
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Objectively characterises and monitors the chiasmal field defect; baseline before decompression.
Excludes pregnancy as a cause of amenorrhoea/galactorrhoea and is needed before dopamine-agonist therapy — but must not delay emergency imaging.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Breast imaging (mammography/ultrasound) for the nipple discharge
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Bilateral milky discharge with central neurological signs is endocrine, not local breast pathology — routine breast imaging would be an anchoring error and must not divert from the emergency.
